methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $5.0B - $10.0B.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $7.6B

data.narrative:
Nuro, a Series G autonomous delivery drone company founded by Dave Ferguson and Jiajun Zhu, commands a current post-money valuation of $6.0B against $2.2B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of three companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector spanning similar valuation tiers: Joby Aviation (public, $10.0B), Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), and Archer Aviation (public, $5.0B).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from the last Series G post-money valuation, yielding $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the three comparables produces $7.6B, with a range spanning $5.0B to $10.0B. These approaches converge tightly, suggesting limited methodological divergence.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6.4B (low) to $8.7B (high), with a median of $7.5B. This represents modest upside from the current $6.0B valuation, implying market pricing near the lower quartile of peer multiples. Key uncertainties include execution risk on autonomous regulatory approval timelines, unit economics at scale, and whether comparable public-market multiples remain sustainable given capital intensity and long commercialization cycles in this sector. The narrow peer set and absence of established profitability metrics across comparables limit conviction in range boundaries.
